challenges.prompt: Complete this anchor tag. Fill in the attribute that tells the browser where to go, and the value that safely opens external links in a new tab.
challenges.explanation: Whenever you add target="_blank" to open a link in a new tab, pair it with rel="noopener noreferrer". Without it, the newly opened page can reach window.opener and redirect your original page to a malicious URL — a classic phishing loophole.

challenges.prompt: This link should jump to the section below when clicked. Fill in the href value and the matching attribute on the section so they connect.
challenges.explanation: A fragment link works in two steps: give the destination a unique id, then point the link's href at # followed by that same id. Clicking href="#contact" scrolls the page to the element whose id="contact".

challenges.prompt: This link is meant to point to another website, but it goes nowhere. What's wrong?
options: An anchor cannot link to an external site without target="_blank"
The href is missing the https:// protocol, so it is treated as a relative path
The link text must exactly match the domain name
You must use <link> instead of <a> for external sites
challenges.explanation: An absolute URL to another site needs the full address including the protocol: href="https://developer.mozilla.org". Without https://, the browser reads it as a relative path and looks for a file named developer.mozilla.org next to the current page.
